sections.paragraph:
μόλις (=Hom. μόγις, fr. μόγος ‘toil’) adv. ‘scarcely’ Trag., Thu.+; PTebt 19, 10 [114 b.c.]; PRyl 113, 27; POxy 1117, 19; PGiss 4, 15; LXX; TestAbr A 11 p. 90, 12 [Stone p. 28]; TestJob, EpArist, Philo, Joseph.; Tat. 39, 1 v.l.—B-D-F §33; Rob. 296; s. μόγις).

① pert. to being hard to accomplish, with difficulty (Lycophron vs. 757; Appian, Liby. 3 §14, Bell. Civ. 1, 8 §33; 1, 77 §351; Ael. Aristid. 48, 43 K.=24 p. 476 D.; Wsd 9:16=μετὰ πόνου; Sir 29:6; Philo, Op. M. 80; Jos., Bell. 1, 149) Lk 9:39 v.l.; Ac 14:18; 23:29 v.l.; 27:7f, 16; 1 Pt 4:18 (Pr 11:31; cp. Artem. 1, 2 p. 4, 13 μόλις ἐσώθη).

② pert. to rarity on a scale of occurrences, not readily, only rarely (Nicander, Ther. 281; Synes., Prov. 1, 11 p. 101d: μόλις [seldom, scarcely ever] do virtue and good fortune meet; Sir 21:20; 26:29; 32:7; Jos., C. Ap. 1, 66, Vi. 173) Ro 5:7; another probability here is

③ pert. to rarity on a scale of expectation, ordinarily not (Phlegon: 257 Fgm. 36, 1; 3 Jac.; Achilles Tat. 2, 26, 1) or scarcely (Appian, Bell. Civ. 3, 53 §218 ὀλίγοι μόλις=scarcely a few).—DELG. M-M. TW.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Lycophron Lycophron , trag., IV–III b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Nicander Nicander , III/II b.c.—List 5

Synes Synes , IV–V a.d.—List 5

Phlegon Phlegon , II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Achilles Tat Achilles Tat , IV a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 657
